Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Nov-22-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Machaela Hoctor of the Office of the City Attorney, 554-3850, appearing for plaintiff and moving party
Nina Wilder of Weinberg & Wilder, 431-3472, appearing for defendant intervenor and opposing party
Damone Hale, 368-8702, appearing for defendant intervenor and opposing party

Argued and the Court granted a Preliminary Injunction against defendant Oakdale Mob and members of the Oakdale Mob to which the City provided evidence of membership to the Court (subject to modification). The Preliminary Injunction is to include conditions of the proposed order with the following exceptions: 1) The curfew clause will be a no loitering clause between midnight and 5:30 am; and 2) Paragraph 3 will add an explicit statement that includes without limitation, that the city may include or remove named individuals. As to the Do Not Associate clause, each side is to submit a proposal to the Court addressing its concerns. A revised form of order that incorporates the changes is due by November 28, 2006. The Temporary Restraining Order will remain in effect. Defendant's request for continuance was denied. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600
